preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: startups seeking an ai-first, intent-driven design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Always first. Establishes context for Explorer-archetype startup founders: frames MODE as the AI-first, intent-driven design system they've been searching for, setting the discovery tone.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Archetype guidance requires Value for Explorer. After orientation, Explorers want to understand capabilities and outcomes before seeing hard proof — this satisfies their discovery mindset by showcasing what MODE can do.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Archetype guidance requires Credibility for Explorer. Trust signals and social proof from peer startups validate the value claims just made, building confidence without feeling pushy.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Decision-stage funnel requires Evidence before Conversion. Concrete metrics, case study data, and proof points give the Explorer the substantive depth they need to feel informed rather than sold to.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Decision-stage funnel requires Decision before Conversion. Presents pricing and plan comparison in a clear, low-pressure frame — letting the Explorer weigh options on their own terms.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Must appear immediately before Recovery. A clear but non-aggressive CTA aligned with Explorer archetype — inviting action as a natural next step in their discovery journey rather than a hard close.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always last. Provides secondary paths (docs, community, demo booking) for Explorers who aren't ready to commit yet, ensuring no visitor hits a dead end.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Features
sections.rationale: Archetype guidance requires Value for Explorer. After orientation, Explorers want to understand capabilities and outcomes before seeing hard proof — this satisfies their discovery mindset by showcasing what MODE can do.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision-stage funnel requires conversion focus. Per notes: 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.' This removes friction at the critical conversion moment.

Explorer archetype paired with decision stage demands deeper engagement. Notes specify: 'Explorer variant uses 2-col-heavy with longer copy per feature,' enabling explorers to understand intent-driven capabilities through extended narrative before converting.

slots.headline: Every dimension of intent. Resolved automatically.
slots.subhead: MODE's resolution engine reads archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode — then makes the right call on behavior, emphasis, and copy register without manual intervention.

features.title: Semantic resolution engine
features.description: Components don't just render — they reason. The four-dimension model resolves behavior across archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode simultaneously, so the right experience surfaces by default rather than by configuration.

features.title: IA planner
features.description: Go from a one-paragraph brief to a full page architecture in seconds. Powered by Claude Opus, the planner maps the right section sequence, component selection, and zone affinity for your specific funnel stage and audience — before you write a single line.

features.title: Multi-variant generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. No separate prompts, no duplicated effort — the resolution engine handles the divergence and the output is immediately renderable in the Next.js preview UI.

features.title: Palette map
features.description: Visual emphasis isn't assigned manually — it accumulates semantically. The palette map resolves per component per funnel stage, so the right elements carry weight at the right moments on the path to conversion, without a designer touching every instance.

features.title: Component manifest
features.description: Eleven components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent. Built on Radix UI primitives, which means zero new mental models — just a richer intelligence layer on top of foundations your team already knows.

features.title: Token dashboard
features.description: Editable palette map, accent configuration, and preset switching in one place. Three presets ship out of the box — funnel-driven, archetype-driven, and feature-emphasis — and the configuration lives in your codebase, not a vendor dashboard.

page.reasoning: Decision-stage conversion page requires footer recovery mechanism. Per notes: 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.' This captures users about to leave before converting.
